text.en: Perform firm bimanual fundal massage; help uterus contract.
Encourage the woman to empty bladder; catheterise if trained.
Call 108 — request obstetric ambulance with blood-bank linked facility.
Communicate to PHC/CHC: prepare oxytocin / methergine / TXA per Dakshata PPH protocol.
Keep the mother warm; raise her legs; record pad count and BP every 5 minutes.

rules.rationale: Estimated blood loss >= 500 mL after vaginal delivery (or saturated pads / clinical signs of shock) defines primary PPH per Dakshata; bimanual massage + uterotonics + transfer save lives.

text.en: Refer to PHC/CHC today for uterine assessment and possible retained products of conception.
Record BP, pulse, temperature; watch for signs of sepsis.

rules.rationale: Secondary PPH commonly indicates retained products or endometritis and requires same-day facility-based assessment.

text.en: Refer to FRU/CHC for IV antibiotics and obstetric review.
Monitor temperature, pulse, BP every 15 minutes en route.

rules.rationale: Maternal temperature >= 38.5 °C plus uterine tenderness or foul discharge in the first 42 postpartum days is presumed puerperal sepsis and needs IV antibiotics within an hour.
